Class RegionSelector

java.lang.Object
sudokusolver.sudoku.RegionSelector
public class RegionSelector extends Object
A parser designed to map ASCII-art grid representations into lists of cells, edges, and corners grouped by character identifiers.

This class allows developers to visually layout complex, non-standard Sudoku regions or geometric constraints using simple text blocks.

Cells-only mode Treats every single character as a Cell. Newlines advance the row counter. 

String extraRegionsText = """
        ......
        .AA...
        .AA.BB
        .AA.BB
        ....BB
        ......
        """;
RegionSelector selector = new RegionSelector(extraRegionsText);
List<Cell> regionA = selector.getCellRegion('A');
sudoku.addRule(new AllDifferentRule(regionA));

Cells+Edges+Corners mode The parity (odd/even) of the current character coordinates determines the element type::

  • (even, even) → Maps character to a Cell.
  • (even, odd) → Maps character to a vertical Edge (right boundary).
  • (odd, even) → Maps character to a horizontal Edge (bottom boundary).
  • (odd, odd) → Maps character to a Corner (bottom-right vertex).
String sudokuText = """
        1|.V.|.|.|.
        -+-+-+-+-+V
        .|.X.|.V.|.
        -+-+-O-+-+-
        .|.V3|.|.|5
        X+-+-+V+-+-
        E|.|.|.|.V.
        -+-O-+-+-+V
        .|E|.|.|.|.
        V+-+V+-+V+-
        .|5|.|3|1|.
        """;
RegionSelector selector = new RegionSelector(sudokuText, 0, 0, true);
Sudoku sudoku = new Sudoku(selector.toBoard(), 6);
List<Edge> vEdges = selector.getEdgeRegion('V');
List<Edge> xEdges = selector.getEdgeRegion('X');
sudoku.addRule(new XVRule(vEdges, xEdges));
List<Cell> evenCells = selector.getCellRegion('E');
sudoku.addRule(new EvenRule(evenCells));
List<Corner> oCorners = selector.getCornerRegion('O');

Note: In examples '.', '-', '|' and '+' are used for visual clarity but are treated the same way as any other character.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    RegionSelector(String s)
    Constructs a RegionSelector by parsing the provided ASCII-art string with top-left corner as the origin (0,0) in cells-only mode.
    RegionSelector(String s, boolean includeEdgesAndCorners)
    Constructs a RegionSelector by parsing the provided ASCII-art string with top-left corner as the origin (0,0).
    RegionSelector(String s, int topRow, int leftCol)
    Constructs a RegionSelector by parsing the provided ASCII-art string in cells-only mode.
    RegionSelector(String s, int topRow, int leftCol, boolean includeEdgesAndCorners)
    Constructs a RegionSelector by parsing the provided ASCII-art string.
    RegionSelector(String s, Cell topLeft)
    Constructs a RegionSelector by parsing the provided ASCII-art string in cells-only mode.
    RegionSelector(String s, Cell topLeft, boolean includeEdgesAndCorners)
    Constructs a RegionSelector by parsing the provided ASCII-art string.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    List<Cell>
    getCellRegion(char c)
    Returns the list of cells in the region represented by the character c.
    List<Cell>
    getCellRegion(String s)
    Returns the list of cells in the regions represented by the characters in the string s.
    List<Corner>
    getCornerRegion(char c)
    Returns the list of corners in the region represented by the character c.
    List<Corner>
    getCornerRegion(String s)
    Returns the list of corners in the regions represented by the characters in the string s.
    List<Edge>
    getEdgeRegion(char c)
    Returns the list of edges in the region represented by the character c.
    List<Edge>
    getEdgeRegion(String s)
    Returns the list of edges in the regions represented by the characters in the string s.
    static List<Cell>
    rectangle(int topRow, int leftCol, int bottomRow, int rightCol)
    Returns the list of cells in the rectangle defined by the top-left corner (topRow, leftCol) and the bottom-right corner (bottomRow, rightCol).
    static List<Cell>
    rectangle(Cell topLeft, Cell bottomRight)
    Returns the list of cells in the rectangle defined by the top-left corner and the bottom-right corner.
    static List<Cell>
    sequence(int startRow, int startCol, int dr, int dc, int length)
    Returns the list of cells in the sequence defined by the starting cell (startRow, startCol) and the direction (dr, dc) for the given length.
    static List<Cell>
    sequence(Cell start, int dr, int dc, int length)
    Returns the list of cells in the sequence defined by the starting cell (start) and the direction (dr, dc) for the given length.
    Board
    toBoard()
    Uses '1'-'9' regions to create a Board where cells in '1' are set to 1, cells in '2' are set to 2, etc.
    Board
    toBoard(int maxNumber)
    Uses regions represeting numbers up to maxNumber to create a Board.

  • Constructor Details

    • RegionSelector

      public RegionSelector(String s, int topRow, int leftCol, boolean includeEdgesAndCorners)
      Constructs a RegionSelector by parsing the provided ASCII-art string.
      Parameters:
      s - ASCII-art string
      topRow - the starting row offset for the layout
      leftCol - the starting column offset for the layout
      includeEdgesAndCorners - true to enable parsing for cells, edges, and corners; false to parse in cells-only mode

    • RegionSelector

      public RegionSelector(String s, Cell topLeft, boolean includeEdgesAndCorners)
      Constructs a RegionSelector by parsing the provided ASCII-art string.
      Parameters:
      s - ASCII-art string
      topLeft - the top-left corner of the layout
      includeEdgesAndCorners - true to enable parsing for cells, edges, and corners; false to parse in cells-only mode

    • RegionSelector

      public RegionSelector(String s, int topRow, int leftCol)
      Constructs a RegionSelector by parsing the provided ASCII-art string in cells-only mode.
      Parameters:
      s - ASCII-art string
      topRow - the starting row offset for the layout
      leftCol - the starting column offset for the layout

    • RegionSelector

      public RegionSelector(String s, Cell topLeft)
      Constructs a RegionSelector by parsing the provided ASCII-art string in cells-only mode.
      Parameters:
      s - ASCII-art string
      topLeft - the top-left corner of the layout

    • RegionSelector

      public RegionSelector(String s)
      Constructs a RegionSelector by parsing the provided ASCII-art string with top-left corner as the origin (0,0) in cells-only mode.
      Parameters:
      s - ASCII-art string

    • RegionSelector

      public RegionSelector(String s, boolean includeEdgesAndCorners)
      Constructs a RegionSelector by parsing the provided ASCII-art string with top-left corner as the origin (0,0).
      Parameters:
      s - ASCII-art string
      includeEdgesAndCorners - true to enable parsing for cells, edges, and corners; false to parse in cells-only mode

  • Method Details

    • getCellRegion

      public List<Cell> getCellRegion(char c)
      Returns the list of cells in the region represented by the character c. If the character is not found, returns an empty list.
      Parameters:
      c - the character representing the region
      Returns:
      the list of cells in the region

    • getCellRegion

      public List<Cell> getCellRegion(String s)
      Returns the list of cells in the regions represented by the characters in the string s. If a character is not found, it is ignored. The resulting list is the concatenation of the lists for each character in s in the order they appear in s.
      Parameters:
      s - the string representing the regions
      Returns:
      the list of cells in the regions

    • getEdgeRegion

      public List<Edge> getEdgeRegion(char c)
      Returns the list of edges in the region represented by the character c. If the character is not found, returns an empty list.
      Parameters:
      c - the character representing the region
      Returns:
      the list of edges in the region

    • getEdgeRegion

      public List<Edge> getEdgeRegion(String s)
      Returns the list of edges in the regions represented by the characters in the string s. If a character is not found, it is ignored. The resulting list is the concatenation of the lists for each character in s in the order they appear in s.
      Parameters:
      s - the string representing the regions
      Returns:
      the list of edges in the regions

    • getCornerRegion

      public List<Corner> getCornerRegion(char c)
      Returns the list of corners in the region represented by the character c. If the character is not found, returns an empty list.
      Parameters:
      c - the character representing the region
      Returns:
      the list of corners in the region

    • getCornerRegion

      public List<Corner> getCornerRegion(String s)
      Returns the list of corners in the regions represented by the characters in the string s. If a character is not found, it is ignored. The resulting list is the concatenation of the lists for each character in s in the order they appear in s.
      Parameters:
      s - the string representing the regions
      Returns:
      the list of corners in the regions

    • toBoard

      public Board toBoard()
      Uses '1'-'9' regions to create a Board where cells in '1' are set to 1, cells in '2' are set to 2, etc. Cells in other regions are involved in the puzzle with no assigned value.
      Returns:
      a Board representing the regions defined by '1'-'9' characters

    • toBoard

      public Board toBoard(int maxNumber)
      Uses regions represeting numbers up to maxNumber to create a Board. Uses '1'-'9', 'A'-'Z', and 'a'-'z' regions to create a Board where cells in '1' are set to 1, cells in '2' are set to 2, ..., cells in '9' are set to 9, cells in 'A' are set to 10, ..., cells in 'Z' are set to 35, cells in 'a' are set to 10, ..., cells in 'z' are set to 35. Cells in other regions are involved in the puzzle with no assigned value.

      Example: If maxNumber is 16, the method will consider characters '1'-'9', 'A'-'G', 'a'-'g' to represent numbers 1-16. Note: If maxNumber is greater than 35, the method will only consider characters up to 'z' (representing 35).

      Parameters:
      maxNumber - the maximum number to be represented by regions
      Returns:
      a board representing the regions defined by '1'-'9', 'A'-'Z', and 'a'-'z' characters

    • rectangle

      public static List<Cell> rectangle(int topRow, int leftCol, int bottomRow, int rightCol)
      Returns the list of cells in the rectangle defined by the top-left corner (topRow, leftCol) and the bottom-right corner (bottomRow, rightCol).
      Parameters:
      topRow - the top row of the rectangle
      leftCol - the left column of the rectangle
      bottomRow - the bottom row of the rectangle
      rightCol - the right column of the rectangle
      Returns:
      the list of cells in the defined rectangle

    • rectangle

      public static List<Cell> rectangle(Cell topLeft, Cell bottomRight)
      Returns the list of cells in the rectangle defined by the top-left corner and the bottom-right corner.
      Parameters:
      topLeft - the top-left corner of the rectangle
      bottomRight - the bottom-right corner of the rectangle
      Returns:
      the list of cells in the defined rectangle

    • sequence

      public static List<Cell> sequence(int startRow, int startCol, int dr, int dc, int length)
      Returns the list of cells in the sequence defined by the starting cell (startRow, startCol) and the direction (dr, dc) for the given length. For

      Example: If startRow=0, startCol=0, dr=1, dc=1, and length=9 the method will return the list of cells in the main diagonal of a 9x9 board: (0,0), (1,1), ..., (8,8).

      Parameters:
      startRow - the row of the starting cell
      startCol - the column of the starting cell
      dr - the row direction
      dc - the column direction
      length - the length of the sequence
      Returns:
      the list of cells in the defined sequence

    • sequence

      public static List<Cell> sequence(Cell start, int dr, int dc, int length)
      Returns the list of cells in the sequence defined by the starting cell (start) and the direction (dr, dc) for the given length.

      Example: If start=(0,0), dr=1, dc=1, and length=9 the method will return the list of cells in the main diagonal of a 9x9 board: (0,0), (1,1), ..., (8,8).

      Parameters:
      start - the starting cell
      dr - the row direction
      dc - the column direction
      length - the length of the sequence
      Returns:
      the list of cells in the defined sequence
